scope: Authored Blender viewport appearance on the exact GN-VM mesh. This endpoint guide contains no slicer data or G-code toolpath.

geometry.role: A two-point mesh line is converted to a beveled curve and joined with instanced square endpoint markers.

capture.toneMappingInterpretation: The evaluated material is literal white Emission at strength 1. Disabling browser display tone mapping preserves the clipped-white authored result; lighting and the studio environment do not drive this surface.

interpretation: Geometry Nodes overrides the object's mahogany source slot: every evaluated face uses the two-node white emit.002 graph. The browser now follows that evaluated assignment and renders the same unlit white surface. Because the guide projects to only one to three pixels in width, raw IoU, corner averages, and RGB values are dominated by different antialias footprints against the capture key. Both silhouettes cover 100% of the other renderer's surface within one pixel and their one-pixel-dilated IoU is 0.9230. Exact topology, matching bounds, the evaluated material assignment, and mutual one-pixel coverage provide the parity evidence; pixel identity is not claimed.
